seoTitle: How to Show/Hide Visuals With Power BI Bookmarks (2026)
metaDescription: Build interactive toggle buttons in Power BI that swap between overlapping charts using the Selection pane, bookmarks, and button actions.
intro: Cramming too many charts onto one page creates clutter, but Power BI's bookmark and selection tools let you layer visuals and toggle between them with a button click. This guide covers overlaying charts, saving visibility states as bookmarks, and wiring buttons to switch between them cleanly.

steps.heading: Open the Bookmarks and Selection panes
steps.body: Go to the View tab and enable both the Bookmarks pane and the Selection pane to manage visual visibility and saved states.

steps.heading: Overlay your visuals
steps.body: Match the size and position of a secondary chart to your primary visual so they sit exactly on top of each other.

steps.heading: Save each state as a bookmark
steps.body: Use the eye icon in the Selection pane to hide one visual at a time, then click Add in the Bookmarks pane and rename each saved state, making sure to uncheck the Data option so filters aren't affected.

steps.heading: Insert toggle buttons
steps.body: Go to Insert, expand the Buttons drawer, and drop a blank button onto the canvas for each bookmark state.

steps.heading: Wire buttons to bookmarks
steps.body: In the Format button menu, turn on the Action toggle, set the type to Bookmark, and select the corresponding saved state for each button.

faq.question: Why does clicking a bookmark reset my slicer selections?
faq.answer: This happens if the Data option is left enabled when saving the bookmark; disabling it ensures only visual visibility changes, not filter states.

faq.question: Can I use this technique across multiple report pages?
faq.answer: Yes, but you should verify each bookmark respects page-specific logic so it doesn't accidentally navigate users to the wrong page.

faq.question: How do I test my buttons while still editing?
faq.answer: Hold Ctrl and click the button directly on the canvas to trigger its action without leaving edit mode.

faq.question: Do the overlaid visuals need to be identical sizes?
faq.answer: Yes, matching size and position exactly is what makes the swap look seamless rather than shifting the layout.
